message:
test(conformance): expand Python reference fixtures and generator

Add new conformance test fixtures covering additional MCP tool operations
to improve parity testing between the Python reference implementation and
the Rust port. Update generate_fixtures.py with corresponding test
scenario generators for the new fixture entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@
 import asyncio
 import json
 import os
+import subprocess
 import sys
 import uuid
 from pathlib import Path
@@ -61,6 +62,8 @@ def _tokens_to_pointer(tokens: list[str]) -> str:
         "timestamp",
         # Git diff excerpts may embed volatile timestamps.
         "excerpt",
+        # Hook paths depend on temp repo roots.
+        "hook",
     }
 )
 
@@ -106,6 +109,12 @@ def _mk_run_dir() -> Path:
     return run_dir
 
 
+def _init_git_repo(path: Path) -> Path:
+    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(path), "init"], check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
+    return path
+
+
 def _set_legacy_env(run_dir: Path) -> None:
     # Make legacy server deterministic and quiet.
     os.environ.setdefault("APP_ENVIRONMENT", "development")
@@ -224,6 +233,30 @@ async def record_tool(tool_name: str, case_name: str, tool_args: dict[str, Any])
             tool_entry["cases"].append(case)
             return out
 
+        async def record_tool_error(
+            tool_name: str,
+            case_name: str,
+            tool_args: dict[str, Any],
+            message_contains: str,
+        ) -> None:
+            try:
+                await _call_tool(mcp, ctx, tool_name, tool_args)
+            except Exception as exc:
+                msg = str(exc)
+                if message_contains not in msg:
+                    raise RuntimeError(
+                        f"expected error containing {message_contains!r}, got {msg!r}"
+                    ) from exc
+                case = {
+                    "name": case_name,
+                    "input": tool_args,
+                    "expect": {"err": {"message_contains": message_contains}},
+                }
+                tool_entry = tools.setdefault(tool_name, {"cases": []})
+                tool_entry["cases"].append(case)
+                return
+            raise RuntimeError(f"expected error for tool {tool_name} case {case_name}")
+
         # --- Tool scenario (ordered) ---------------------------------------------------------
         await record_tool("health_check", "default", {})

+        repo_uninstall = _init_git_repo(repo_root / "repo_uninstall")
